text:
In a small town nestled between rolling hills, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver. He was known for his gentle wisdom and his nightly lectures on the secrets of the forest.

One tranquil evening, as twilight painted the sky with shades of orange and purple, Oliver decided it was a perfect night for teaching a new young owl named Luna. He told her about the great circle of life, how every creature has its place, and how the stars guide them through darkness.

Luna listened intently, her small feathers ruffled by the magic of stories. Oliver showed her constellations—the wise old fox, the jovial lion, and the steadfast wolf. He explained how even stars seem to move because they're so far away, but it's really just our perception.

As the moon took its vigil above, Oliver paused. He knew this was important to Luna. "Sometimes," he said softly, "we feel lost. But you're not alone. Just take a deep breath, look up
